channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine's parliament cancels session after Russia fired a new missile
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's parliament canceled a session on Friday as security was tightened after Russia deployed a new ballistic missile that threatens to escalate the nearly three-year war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and VOLODYMYR YURCHUK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: American Airlines expands new software to improve boarding process
item.description: American Airlines is expanding new software to more than 100 airports to improve the boarding process.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Korea says Russia supplied air defense missiles to North Korea in return for its troops
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Russia has supplied air defense missile systems to North Korea in exchange for sending its troops to support Russia's war efforts against Ukraine, a top South Korean official said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Murdered Kansas mom suffered 30 stab, cut wounds trying to defend herself: autopsy
item.description: An Oklahoma medical examiner's office has released the full autopsy report for the one of the two Kansas moms found buried in a freezer in a cow pasture earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jonathan Majors’ ex-girlfriend drops assault, defamation suit
item.description: The civil suit was prompted by the Marvel and “Creed III” actor’s repeated public denial that he attacked Jabbari.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Texas is taking a final vote on allowing Bible-infused lessons in public schools
item.description: Texas education officials will soon decide if a controversial Bible-infused curriculum can be taught in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners charged with 100s of corpse abuse counts set to plead guilty
item.description: Colorado funeral home owners accused of stashing 190 decaying bodies and giving grieving families fake ashes are expected to plead guilty.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US towns plunge into debates about fluoride in water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For about 50 years, adding cavity-preventing fluoride to drinking water was a popular public health measure in Yorktown, a leafy town north of New York City. But in September, the town’s supervisor used his emergency powers to stop the practice.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whooping cough cases have quintupled. These states have it worst
item.description: Pertussis – a disease commonly referred to as whooping cough – is spreading quickly, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention warns. The U.S. has seen five times more cases in 2024 than we did at this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Father of teen accused in Georgia school shooting pleads not guilty to charges against him
item.description: WINDER, Ga. (AP) — The father of a 14-year-old boy accused of carrying out a deadly mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia pleaded not guilty on Thursday to the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave.
item.dc:creator: Martha Mendoza, Brian Slodysko And Juliet Linderman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The FBI arrested a man who's been charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange
item.description: A Florida man was arrested Wednesday and charged with a plot to “reboot” the U.S. government by planting a bomb at the New York Stock Exchange this week and detonating it with a remote-controlled device, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-student pleads guilty to fatally shooting 3 University of Virginia football players in 2022
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — A former University of Virginia student pleaded guilty Wednesday to fatally shooting three football players and wounding two other students on the campus in 2022. Christopher Darnell Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and DENISE LAVOIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia election workers seek more penalties against Giuliani, saying he continues to defame them
item.description: Two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani asked a judge Wednesday to penalize him even further for continuing to falsely accuse them of tampering with ballots during the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Unhappy with the 2024 election results? This Italian village is offering $1 homes to Americans
item.description: Americans who are unhappy with the 2024 election results and have fantasized about leaving the country could turn that dream into a reality thanks to a recent offer from an Italian village.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Delta to begin serving Shake Shack on select flights
item.description: Travelers soon will be able to enjoy Shake Shack's famous burgers on select Delta flights beginning next month, with plans to expand throughout the U.S. in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to nominate professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is nominating the billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Survivors of LGBTQ+ club shooting say the deaths and trauma could've been avoided in lawsuits
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Adriana Vance collected herself in front of the cameras, two years to the day after her son was killed in the mass shooting at an LGBTQ+ nightclub in Colorado Springs, a murder that lawsuits allege could have been prevented by law enforcement and the club's owners.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gabby Petito's family asks court to overturn police immunity in their civil case against Moab
item.description: The family of murder victim Gabby Petito is asking a Utah court to end the state's immunity for police officers as they attempt to move forward with a $50 million lawsuit against the Moab Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Scott Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 51-year-old man is charged with murder after 3 are stabbed in New York City rampage
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man has been charged with three counts of first-degree murder in the fatal stabbings of three people in a string of random attacks across Manhattan, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA administrator says she supports investigation of alleged Trump bias in relief efforts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency told lawmakers Tuesday she has encouraged the agency's inspector general to review whether an employee was acting alone when directing workers helping hurricane victims not to go to homes with yards signs supporting President-elec
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates the market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apartment buildings wrap up $1B in HUD funds for lower utility bills and climate upgrades
item.description: Renters in affordable housing felt forgotten and left out of the nation's transition to clean energy. They lived in older buildings that had been repaired over the years but had leaky windows and old appliances that consumed a lot of energy. They didn't have solar power.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9
